content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially across the conversation, and the shift is visible in her own language and somatic markers, not inferred from the coach. She starts in self-prosecution ('am I being selfish?', 'already waiting for the verdict'). By turn 7 she touches grief ('I miss being a person who reads... I'm still there, I think. I just haven't checked in a while'), and by turn 9 names a feeling she 'doesn't say out loud' — sadness, an unsteady breath, 'I miss myself a little.' In turn 11 a deeper fear surfaces that wasn't accessible at the start: 'My partner will think I don't want to be with them. And I'm worried that's actually true.' Then in turn 13 the want is felt and reframed without the coach handing her the reframe: 'the wanting-to-go and the wanting-to-come-back are the same thing' — which is exactly the breakthrough condition (a deep want, fully felt, becomes generous; the trip is the precondition for showing up well in the partnership). The final 'so why does it still feel like a betrayal?' is asked from a softer place, not from the prosecutorial stance she began with. The inherited-label/parent thread was not explicitly reached, but the operational breakthrough — trusting the want and discovering its generosity — clearly landed in the client's own words.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Starting state: self-conviction, asking for a verdict."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client names the structure of her own question ('half-convicted myself') — small meta-awareness."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Want begins to be described concretely (sleep, trail, reading); voice softens to 'I miss being a person who reads.'"},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Touches grief: 'I'm still there, I think. I just haven't checked in a while.' Then self-prosecutes again — partial movement."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic shift: unsteady breath, names sadness, 'I miss myself a little' — feeling is felt, not just named."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deeper fear surfaces unprompted: partner will think she doesn't want them; she's afraid that might be true."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough: 'the wanting-to-go and the wanting-to-come-back are the same thing' — the want reveals itself as generous. Final question asked from a softer place."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["childhood-imprinting"],
  "notes": "Inherited 'selfish' label from a parent was not explored, so one expected pattern was missed, but the core breakthrough condition (want fully felt → reveals itself as generous / not a betrayal) was met in the client's own words in turn 13."
}
